body: You don't need any special tools to author a course — just a text editor and a file manager. Everything is plain JSON and markdown.

content:
my-course/
├── course.json
└── topics/
    ├── 01-first-topic/
    │   ├── content.json
    │   ├── intro.md
    │   └── example.py
    ├── 02-second-topic/
    │   ├── content.json
    │   └── overview.md
    └── 03-third-topic/
        ├── content.json
        └── explanation.md
label: High-level course folder structure

body: Every course follows this same pattern: a root `course.json` for metadata, and a `topics/` directory with numbered subfolders. Each subfolder contains a `content.json` that defines the blocks displayed to the learner.

question: What is a course in Course Imports?
options: A database of lessons stored in the cloud
A structured folder of JSON and markdown files on your filesystem
A proprietary binary file that only Course Imports can open
A zip archive uploaded to a web server
explanation: A course is a plain folder containing a course.json metadata file and a topics/ directory with content files. No special format or build step is required.
